No retrieved primary study causally addresses proximal TCR inhibition, an inhibitory checkpoint response, anergy or hyporesponsiveness. paper_assessments.experimental_system: human; PBMCs and RA synovial tissue (GEO dataset); no specific T cell subset
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: RBPJ expression was significantly lower in RA patients and negatively correlated with disease activity.
paper_assessments.rationale: Expression correlation and GO/KEGG enrichment only; no perturbation and no inhibitory-signalling readout. TCR pathway appears merely as an enriched term.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: mature T cells (species not specified in passage); gene-regulation cell model
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: RBPJ depletion leads to upregulation of many Notch target genes.
paper_assessments.rationale: Perturbational study of RBPJ gene-regulatory function (Notch target genes, IL2ra), but addresses transcriptional control, not TCR inhibition or an inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and mouse HCC; CD8+ tumor-infiltrating T cells; patient-derived xenografts
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: degradation of RBPJ, thereby inhibiting T cell exhaustion through the IRF4/TNFRSF1B axis.
paper_assessments.rationale: Causal: reducing RBPJ (via USP7 degradation) alleviates CD8+ exhaustion. Exhaustion is inhibitory, but impaired/attenuated TCR function is not explicitly demonstrated.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and mouse HCC; CD8+ T cells; in vitro and in vivo
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Inhibiting RBPJ with RIN1 alleviates T cell exhaustion and enhances anti-tumor immunity.
paper_assessments.rationale: Perturbational: RBPJ-complex inhibitor RIN1 reverses CD8+ exhaustion with functional killing readouts, but proximal TCR signalling attenuation is not explicitly shown.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: live-cell biophysics (non-immune cell model); DNA-binding kinetics
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: The study tracked RBPJ binding kinetics and transcriptional activity in live cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: Biophysical characterization of RBPJ-DNA binding kinetics; no T cell system and no inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Treg cells require Rbpj to specifically restrain TH2 responses.
paper_assessments.rationale: Causal Treg-specific Rbpj deletion, but the phenotype is TH2 lineage restraint/differentiation, not TCR inhibition, checkpoint, anergy or hyporesponsiveness.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: mouse and human decidua/placenta; non-immune stromal cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: circ-Hdac4 regulates decidualization through the miR-30c-RBPJ axis.
paper_assessments.rationale: RBPJ studied in decidualization and preeclampsia; non-immune system with no relevance to T cells.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: RBPJ-dependent Notch signaling initiates the T cell program in thymus-seeding progenitors.
paper_assessments.rationale: Addresses T cell lineage specification/commitment via Notch, not any inhibitory or TCR-attenuating phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and mouse; monocyte-derived macrophages (non-T immune)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: RBPJ is required for pro-inflammatory macrophage activation in thoracic aortic dissection.
paper_assessments.rationale: RBPJ function studied in macrophage activation/glycolysis; non-T-cell system, unrelated to TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: RBPJ is frequently deleted in human cancers and acts as a tumor suppressor.
paper_assessments.rationale: Focuses on RBPJ loss and tumorigenesis via Notch-independent derepression; no T cell or inhibitory-phenotype content.

parsed.tier_rationale: Two independent primary experimental studies (PMC12068301, PMC9887061) provide direct perturbational evidence that RBPJ maintains/promotes CD8+ T cell exhaustion in HCC, with functional anti-tumor readouts. However, neither explicitly demonstrates impaired or attenuated TCR signalling/function; per the mandatory rule, exhaustion alone is insufficient for moderate. No retrieved primary study causally addresses proximal TCR inhibition, an inhibitory checkpoint response, anergy or hyporesponsiveness. Remaining papers are computational/associative, generic T cell development/differentiation, or non-T-cell systems. With relevant evidence gated below moderate and the lower-tier rule applied, the tier is weak.
